inputs.name: dragoverPredicate
inputs.description:
Predicate function that checks whether the item can be dragged over another item.
If the function returns `false`, dragged over item will not be highlighted, and drop event will be canceled.

inputs.name: dropMode
inputs.description:
Defines drop strategy:
* `shift` mode will create line after the closest drop element.
* `group` mode will create replace indicator on a whole closest drop element.
* `auto` mode will create a line after the closest drop element
if dragged element coordinates are shifted for 30% from the center of the closest drop element.
Otherwise, it will create replace indicator on a whole closest drop element.

`shift` mode is the default.

inputs.name: dropPredicate
inputs.description: Predicate function that checks whether the item can be dropped over another item.

inputs.name: gridMode
inputs.description:
Defines if the element is allowed to be dragged in 2 dimensions,
When true - replace indicator will be displayed vertically

inputs.name: items
inputs.description: Array of items, that will be sorted

inputs.name: threshold
inputs.description: Threshold of dragged item over another item to define which type of `dropMode` should be used.

outputs.name: dropCancelled
outputs.description: Event that is thrown when predicate function returned false.

outputs.name: dropPredicateCalculating
outputs.description:
Event emitted when `dropPredicate` function evaluation changes.
Emits `true` when evaluation started, and `false` when completed.

outputs.name: itemDropped
outputs.description: Event that is thrown when the item is dropped

outputs.name: itemsChange
outputs.description: Event that is thrown when items are reordered

methods.name: dragEnd
methods.description: Method called when an element is released

methods.name: dragStart
methods.description: Method called when an element is started to be dragged

methods.name: onMove
methods.description: Method called when the item is being moved by 1 px

methods.name: refreshQueryList
methods.description: Refreshes the indexes of the items.
